tactical_shape: 5-4-1, invited opponents deep and packed the middle
bunker-and-counter approach with speculative early balls wide
relied on deep defending, physicality, and breaks rather than sustained possession
standout_players: Orlando Mosquera denied Budimir and made a rebound save
Dominik Livakovic was the main obstacle for Panama's attacks
José Fajardo was isolated up front and outnumbered
Amir Murillo created Panama's best first-half chance with a through ball and cross
José Luis Rodríguez's header was saved/deflected onto the bar
home_read.momentum: Eliminated and still without a point at this World Cup; the article frames the side as proud, physical, but limited by lack of attacking quality.

tactical_shape: Sought to play through Panama's compact block and used crosses when that failed
Counter-counterattacked when Panama broke, then attacked space after the goal
Second-half width from Josip Stanisic's overlap and Marco Pasalic's through ball produced the winner
standout_players: Ante Budimir scored the only goal after coming on at half-time
Luka Modric orchestrated play, was celebrated for his 200th senior cap, and subbed off to a hero's ovation after 81 minutes
Josip Stanisic provided the overlap and cross for the goal
Marco Pasalic supplied the through ball and later had a shot/attempt on the break
Dominik Livakovic made several reliable close-range saves
fatigue_injuries: Luka Modric is 40 and was subbed off after 81 minutes
away_read.momentum: A needed win after the opening defeat, leaving Croatia a point behind the leaders and able to move on with renewed relief and confidence.
